No Poverty.reason: No explicit course content or learning outcomes directly address poverty eradication, social protection, or economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course does not contain any content related to hunger, food security, nutrition, or sustainable agriculture practices.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: There is no evidence in the course description or objectives related to health, well-being, or medical services.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education (4.3) and develops essential communication, problem-solving, and entrepreneurial skills for employment and professional growth (4.4).
evidence: enhance students’ oral communication skills in business contexts
researching brand stories and establishing their own
be ready to participate in discussions on current business issues

Gender Equality.reason: The course syllabus does not contain any specific content, objectives, or activities related to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No course content or learning outcomes are related to water quality, sanitation, or sustainable water management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course does not address energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency in any capacity.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly promotes entrepreneurship (8.3) through business formation tasks and develops professional communication skills essential for decent work and productive employment (8.5).
evidence: enhance students’ oral communication skills in business contexts and professional settings
lead students to the formation of business

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course directly supports entrepreneurship and the formation of new businesses, aligning with fostering small-scale enterprise growth and innovation (9.3, 9.5).
evidence: formation of business by researching brand stories and establishing their own
be able to make a company profile and business briefing

Reduced Inequalities.reason: By focusing on global business cultures and cross-cultural communication, the course indirectly promotes understanding and inclusion, contributing to reduced inequalities (10.2).
evidence: be familiar with global business cultures
the business cultures around the world

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: There is no content in the syllabus related to urban development, infrastructure, or community sustainability.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competence B explicitly requires students to understand and identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues, directly linking to responsible business practices (12.6, 12.8).
evidence: B. 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, there is no specific focus on climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness.

Life Below Water.reason: The course does not include any content related to marine life, ocean conservation, or sustainable use of marine resources.

Life on Land.reason: There is no evidence in the syllabus concerning terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Debating skills promote participatory decision-making (16.7), and identifying ethics (Core Competence B) contributes to accountable institutions and reduced corruption (16.5, 16.6).

identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course develops communication, teamwork, and cross-cultural understanding, which are crucial for effective global and multi-stakeholder partnerships (17.9, 17.16).
